title: How marketers are using Google Antigravity
summary: Google Antigravity is an agent-first software development platform that enables developers to build, orchestrate, and monitor AI agents. It connects AI agents to code editors, terminals, and browsers to perform development tasks autonomously and is being expanded for use by marketers.
writeup: Patrick Thomas, head of executive strategy and narratives for consumer apps marketing at Google, describes using an internal tool called Google Antigravity as a writing partner that learns his tone of voice and workflows over time. The video is a short first-person testimonial aimed at non-technical professionals, emphasizing that the tool requires no coding background and operates through a chat interface. Its central claim is that AI can codify years of hard-won experience and extend individual impact without replacing existing skills.
key_points: Google Antigravity is an internal AI productivity tool Google uses internally, accessible via a chat interface and requiring no coding ability.

The tool functions as a personalized writing partner: given a writing sample, it reproduces the user's tone of voice, and its usefulness increases as it accumulates knowledge of the user's preferences and workflows.
The speaker frames the value proposition as codifying years of professional expertise into a draft-generating thought partner that can pitch angles based on standing instructions.
The core argument is that AI extends individual impact rather than replacing existing skills, positioning the tool as collaborative rather than substitutive.
